matches.matched_text: Some vote him Vike, some mote him Mike, some dub him Llyn and Phin
matches.snippet: Some vote him Vike, some mote him Mike, some dub him Llyn and Phin while others hail him Lug
matches.reasoning: Phin is a direct garbled variant of Finn; appears in a catalogue of names for the hero HCE

matches.snippet: choir of the O'Daley O'Doyles doublesixing the chorus in Fenn Mac Call and the Serven Feeries
matches.reasoning: Fenn Mac Call is garbled form of Finn MacCool; appears as title of mythic tale-fragment

matches.snippet: Where from? roars Poolbeg. Fingal Mac Oscar Onesine Bargearse Boniface
matches.reasoning: Fingal is direct variant of Fionn/Finn (Irish Fionngheal); Mac Oscar invokes Finn's son Oisin/Ossian from Fenian tradition

matches.matched_text: Humpty Dumpty... fell with a roll and a rumble... curled up like Lord Olofa Crumple By the butt of the Magazine Wall
matches.snippet: Have you heard of one Humpty Dumpty How he fell with a roll and a rumble And curled up like Lord Olofa Crumple By the butt of the Magazine Wall
matches.reasoning: The ballad of Humpty Dumpty mirrors Finnegan's Fall; the great man falling, recumbent by a wall (Dublin terrain motif); implies resurrection/wake

matches.matched_text: He was one time our King of the Castle Now he's kicked about like a rotten old parsnip
matches.snippet: He was one time our King of the Castle Now he's kicked about like a rotten old parsnip
matches.reasoning: Fall from kingship and degradation; Finnegan/HCE motif of former greatness now diminished

matches.matched_text: He was fafafather of all schemes
matches.snippet: He was fafafather of all schemes for to bother us Slow coaches and immaculate contraceptives
matches.reasoning: fafafather invokes Finn/father archetype; the generative, world-making ancestor figure

matches.snippet: doublesixing the chorus in Fenn Mac Call and the Serven Feeries of Loch Neach Galloper Troppler
matches.reasoning: Fianna/Fenians motif: the warrior band and magical dwelling; Seven Feeries = Fenian tradition of Otherworld helpers

matches.matched_text: Before he fell hill he filled heaven: a stream, alplapping streamlet, coyly coiled um, cool of her curls
matches.snippet: Before he fell hill he filled heaven: a stream, alplapping streamlet, coyly coiled um, cool of her curls
matches.reasoning: Fall motif (before he fell); hill/terrain metamorphosis; paradoxical cosmic filling (before falling) invokes Finn's cosmic/landscape body

matches.snippet: Our antheap we sensed as a Hill of Allen, the Barrow for an People, one Jotnursfjaell
matches.reasoning: Hill of Allen: Finn's seat in Irish mythology; Jotnursfjaell: giant's mountain (Norse); metonymic landscape body of the sleeping giant

matches.snippet: Not olderwise Inn the days of the Bygning would our Traveller remote
matches.reasoning: Bygning echoes Bygmester Finnegan (the bricklayer/builder from Finnegan's Wake opening); builder archetype
